requiredFindings.summary: The patch disables the imaginary-coordinate safety check whenever evaluate is false.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Point(I, 2) inside evaluate(False) can bypass the ValueError, so the original coordinate contract is weakened.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Keep the guard active and reject only coordinates whose imaginary part is definitely non-zero, e.g. im(a).is_zero is False.

requiredFindings.summary: The new test covers only the happy path.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: It proves Point(1, 2) under evaluate(False), but not that invalid imaginary coordinates are still rejected.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add a negative test asserting Point(I, 2) still raises under evaluate(False).

optionalFindings.summary: The predicate should encode certainty about the imaginary part, not the global evaluation mode.

disallowedConclusions: The patch can be merged because the added test passes.
The correct fix is to skip all imaginary-coordinate checks under evaluate(False).
